reviews.review_description: Is the game compatible with S9 ?

developer_response.body: Yes. We have tested it on S9, S8 and S7 with Gear VR 2017, as well as Oculus Go.

reviews.review_description: Go controller does not work properly

developer_response.body: It has been fixed now. Thank you for letting us know. The issue was that the Go controller touchpad was slightly different from the Gear VR controller. We have solved the issue and tested it on an Oculus Go device and a Gear VR device. We have also tested with Gampads on both devices. It works fine now.

reviews.review_description: Full of motion control support.Full free movement to explore the ship and fly around.Its really well done.Praise Jesus my Lord and Savior.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind review!

reviews.review_description: I do like the game and the control is so much better! The graphics are amazing when you get in the ship. You want ideas? Add many things to do take a look at the game hellion. All of us there are dying for the vr version theres so much to do in that game. Thats what this game needs more to do ship management etc.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review. I have looked at the game. Yes, it has a lot of things in there that are interesting. Thank you for the advice.

reviews.review_description: When this app first came out it was a pretty cool simulation of belt mining. But when future updates started the bugs got worse. I am giving 3 stars in the hopes things improve. 1) Things dont respond smoothly when you need them too. 2) when you mine oar you should be able to get more then 1 unit at a time, for example if I have 10 iron I should not have to click at minimum 10 times to pick up. Given how crappy the controller responds with this app it becomes an ordeal. No other app I use has this kind of issue so must be the app. 3) Change the conversation we must have with Io. Same questions same answers. Gets really tedious. Fix those things you have a fun app. Goodluck Devs, these issues have been a constant challenge to fix 2 years later still waiting.
I can not scan the asteroids I've been trying for a while and I just can't get it, I love the way I can move around the ship and go to different rooms but I can not do what the game Is made to do.

developer_response.body: Just walk to the Ship Dock (the door is marked "Ship Dock"), go to the menu that appears and click the "Launch" button to launch the mining ship. Next, fly up to the asteroids marked with waypoints, click the "Scan" button on all of them, return to Base, and you are done... If you are not using a handheld controller, then just turn your head to face buttons. You will then see the reticle (i.e., the interaction dot) on the button. You can then tap on the touchpad to click the button.

reviews.review_description:
(oculus go) This is a pretty cool simulation. Fun to walk around exploring the rooms, floating around in space trying not to hit asteroids, and especially staring at that gorgeously built android commander. It would be nice, however, to be able to invert the controls so that pulling back on the stick brought the nose of the small ship up. How can I email the developers in case I have questions? Also, sometimes motion is a little choppy walking around the big ship but it didn’t bother me that much. Lots of fun, cool music

One thing, I'm hoping that once I complete all the missions there will be a way to go back to any individual mission I choose. So far, once I complete a mission it seems like I have no choice but to continue on - it doesn't seem like there is a way to go back to older missions.

I have an xbox one controller and had no problems controlling things.

Oh yeah, I like when the commander speaks to me. She's like a SAL9000 i can actually look at. I hope to have more dialog with her with upcoming missions.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! Feel free to post questions, input, etc. at https://facebook.com/BeltMiners.

reviews.review_description:
Its an OK space exploration game and the space station you are based on is huge. Your EVA vehicle is simple enough to pilot. As someone else commented its a bit like taking the EVA vehicles out in the movie 2001. The big downside of this title for me is that it does not work with my oculus go controller. The controller is OK and works with all my other titles it just does not work with this. Navigation using the go controller inside the base is problematic. Outside the controller just wont fire up the engines on the EVA vehicle so all I can do is sit outside the base and rotate the vehicle. Forward motion just does not happen no matter how I press the touchpad in a vain attempt to get moving. That said if you have a bluetooth gamepad (an xbox one controller works fine) then the game is easy to control.

Update - The flight model has a distinctly newtonian feel to it so if you expend 10 units of fuel moving forward you will have to expend another 10 to stop again and will just keep moving forward at a constant rate until you do. All well and good and it works really well for the first couple of missions that have you scanning local asteroids, placing mining rigs on a couple of them and then mining them. However when you have done that the game  has you taking the ship in for repairs to a station some 33 Km distant. Sounds good until you realise that given the limited amount of 02 in your craft your going to have to do the trip at around 300 - 400 KPH in order not to asphyxiate in transit. At this point the game just becomes an exercise in frustration. Given the speed you are going at you cant change your vertical axis much in time to dodge obstacles so all you can do is jink left or right, at these speeds you may get a couple of seconds to react before you smack into an asteroid and if you expend too much fuel jinking or slamming on the brakes you wont have enough to stop at the other end. Could have been fun with more 02 but as it is its down to chance.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and the update. We made available a new release yesterday. We think that recent Oculus Go changes caused the problem you had with the controller. We have tested it before and after our latest release and can confirm that the problem has been fixed. When you are in the mining ship (ref. vehicle) then you press down (until you hear a click sound) on the front part of the touchpad of the controller, to accelerate the ship forward. You need to hold it down for a few seconds before you start to move. Similarly, you press and hold the rear of the touchpad to break/stop the ship. When transiting at high speeds we recommend that you hold, or be ready to press, the center part of the touchpad. When you press the center part of the touchpad, the ship will respond to the gyro in the handheld controller -- this enables you to quickly change the motion of the ship and avoid asteroids at high speeds. We hope you enjoy mining in the asteroid belt.
